mnemonics.title: su vs su -
mnemonics.content: Think: 'su minus equals MORE' - the minus gives you MORE (full) environment. Without minus, you get LESS (current environment).

mnemonics.title: su -c meaning
mnemonics.content: Think 'C' in -c stands for 'Command' - it runs a single Command.

mnemonics.title: su vs sudo passwords
mnemonics.content:
su = TARGET's password (you need permission from the person you're becoming)
sudo = YOUR password (you have permission already)

acronyms.usage: Used to temporarily switch to another user account, commonly to root for administrative tasks.
Modular system for authentication in Linux, configured in /etc/pam.d/ for su command access control.
Encrypted protocol for secure remote access; avoid using su over unencrypted SSH connections.

visualTips.visualization: Think of su like putting on a different ID badge: you keep your physical location (directory) but your identity (user ID) changes. With 'su -', you also move to their office (home directory) with their supplies (environment).
Draw two boxes: 'su' box shows current PATH, HOME stays same. 'su -' box shows new PATH, HOME changes to target user's home, all new environment variables.

analogies.analogy: su is like borrowing someone else's key to enter a building. sudo is like having your own keycard that says 'also allowed in that room'.
su is like visiting someone's house but keeping your shoes on. su - is like taking a shower and wearing their clothes - full immersion in their environment.
su requires the TARGET user's password, like needing the office manager's permission to use their computer. sudo is like showing your own ID badge that has special privileges.
